commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Glacier View Sea Kayaking
commerceProducts.description: Embark on a scenic sightseeing drive through a historic part of Juneau on your way to North Douglas Island, the launching point of your kayaking excursion. With forested slopes, peaks, and the Mendenhall Glacier as your backdrop, you'll paddle through the waters of Fritz Cove in search of local wildlife. Keep an eye out for eagles, herons, porpoise and even humpback whales. Then enjoy an Alaskan-Style snack before going back to the port.
highlights: Kayak to the Mendenhall Wetlands, a protected habitat that's teeming with more than 40 species of birds and nearly a dozen different mammals.
Explore Fritz Cove and possibly view bald eagles, herons, shorebirds, waterfowl, porpoises and even humpback whales.
Kayak through local waters, getting up-close views of local wildlife and the Mendenhall Glacier eight miles away.
Learn about local flora and fauna, and the history behind landmarks such as Smuggler's Cove. Enjoy a tasty Alaskan snack before boarding the bus back to the port.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Mendenhall Glacier Canoe Paddle and Trek
commerceProducts.description: Experience the consistent and stunning Alaskan views from both land and water on your journey to the awe-inspiring Mendenhall Glacier. Mendenhall Lake is a treasure trove of natural beauty that rests in front of the glacier. Your guide will outfit you with gear, orient you to your canoe and provide a safety briefing before you paddle toward the Mendenhall Glacier. Glide between the towering cliffs and catch a glimpse of some of Alaska's amazing wildlife. Look for white, small, and slender Arctic Tern seabird nest near the lake. Mountain goats appear high in the mountains along the rocky outcroppings. Watch as billies, nannies and kids make their way to the Rock Peninsula jutting out into the lake. Black bears are also seen occasionally in the brush. As you paddle across Mendenhall Lake, you'll pass floating blue-hued icebergs and the powerful Nugget Falls waterfall cascading down the mountainside. When you reach land, trade your paddling gear for backpacks and trekking poles and absorb the beauty of the beautiful thirteen-mile long river of ice. After landing the canoe at the glacier beach, trade out the paddles for trekking poles and begin the hike over the rocky terrain carved out by the glacier. You will get an up-close and intimate perspective of the glacier, while learning about it’s recession, glacier dynamics, and the breathtaking ice features that unfold from the edge. The Mendenhall Glacier is constantly changing and offers a unique experience each day.
highlights: Canoe paddle: Paddle past icebergs, waterfalls and arctic tern nesting cliffs.
Glacier Views and Hike: Step out of the canoe and hike over the rock terrain carved out by the glacier.
Mendenhall Glacier: Get an up close and intimate perspective of the glacier from the edge, all while learning about the recession of the glacier and the wilderness that surrounds it.
Snacks: Enjoy a tasty light snack of trail mix, nuts, cheese and granola bar.

activityLevel.description: This tour is not recommended for guests with pre-existing medical conditions such as cardiac, respiratory, or orthopedic conditions, including joint injuries or disease, or any other serious medical condition. commerceProducts.description: Get ready for an adventure of a lifetime. Follow a secluded trail on a 3.5-hour strenuous hike that takes you high above the Mendenhall Glacier. Depending on the days hiking conditions, your guide will choose the best trail for you to explore. Capture photos of the spectacular views of the Tongass National Forest and along the way watch for wildlife as you venture through the glacier-carved wilderness. Discover Alaska's ever-changing scenery on this unforgettable hiking adventure.

Encounter a variety of wildflowers and wildlife in Tongass National Forest, the world's largest remaining temperate rainforest.
Work up a sweat as you earn the glaciers reward.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Klondike Rock Climbing Adventure
commerceProducts.description: Thrill seekers get their fill on a three-hour outdoor Alaskan adventure that includes a scenic Klondike Highway drive and rainforest nature hike, plus adrenaline-rushing rock climbing at White Pass, with all the necessary gear. A 20-minute van ride on the historic Klondike Highway leads you past picturesque lakes and rivers on a trail that prospectors once used during the 1898 Gold Rush. Hop off and follow your guide on an uphill hike through the rainforest across trails teeming with local plants and wildlife. Then meet your professional climbing guides, who will offer a safety briefing and outfit you with all the essential equipment. Climbing shoes, a helmet, and harness will keep you safe. You can choose from over a dozen climbing routes suited for all ability levels, as no experience is necessary. Then get ready for the thrill of a lifetime as you climb a rock face carved out over time by Alaska's mighty glaciers. Take in sweeping views of the river below during the climb and enjoy light snacks and hot cocoa as a tasty way to cap off the action-packed experience.
highlights: Choose from more than a dozen climbing routes, each one rewarding you with an amazing river view.
No experience required.
Enjoy a driving tour of downtown Skagway and a guided hike to the rock climbing site.
This tour offers great climbs and professional instruction for all abilities and skill levels.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: White Pass Bennett Lake and Yukon Adventure
commerceProducts.description: Experience the breathtaking beauty of the North as you travel into the Yukon on this historic railway & motorcoach adventure. Begin your day by traveling via motorcoach to the White Pass Train Depot where you will board the train for the 67-mile journey into the Yukon. Take the unforgettable journey aboard the "Scenic Railway of the World" as you travel from tidewater to the summit of White Pass, a 2,865-foot elevation gain, and onto beautiful Bennett Lake. Relax in a vintage railcar as you pass Bridal Veil Falls, Inspiration Point and Dead Horse Gulch --This fully narrated tour features panoramic views of mountains, gorges, waterfalls, tunnels, trestles, and historic sites. Accessible only by train, Bennett is the site of an abandoned gold rush town located at the end of the Chilkoot Trail on the shores of Bennett Lake. On arrival in Bennett, step off the train and journey back in time as you explore the White Pass historic interpretive center or take a self-guided walking tour up to the church, the views are stunning. Re-board the train and enjoy a hearty box lunch as you travel along the shores of the lake and onto the village of Carcross. Once in Carcross, you will have time to visit the charming community prior to boarding your motorcoach for the trip down the Klondike Highway and back to your ship in Skagway.
highlights: Relax in a vintage railcar along White Pass & Yukon Route Railway, offering stunning window seat views.
Explore the Yukon communities of Bennett and Carcross.
Enjoy lunch onboard the White Pass Railway.

activityLevel.description: Full participation may require leisurely walking over primarily even surfaces. There may be a limited number of steps, a minimal amount of cobblestones or uneven surfaces, and some standing for extended periods of time.
activityLevel.name: Mild

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Yukon Adventure and White Pass Scenic Railway
commerceProducts.description: Delight in Skagway’s fascinating history and spectacular mountainous scenery as you travel to the White Pass Summit and onward to the border of the Yukon territory. Explore the historic White Pass Trail of '98 from different perspectives. Begin your day ascending into the mountains aboard the White Pass and Yukon Railway. Next, arrive at Fraser, British Columbia, and board a motor coach to continue north through Tutshi Canyon to the border of Yukon territory. Stretch your legs with a photo stop at the "Welcome to Yukon" sign before reboarding your motorcoach.Next arrive at the Yukon Suspension Bridge where you will have time to explore on your own, enjoy a hearty bowl of bison chili and take an optional jaunt across the thrilling suspension bridge for which the venue is named. While at the bridge, experience the sensation of a swaying walkway 57 feet above the rapids of the Tutshi River. Enjoy the scenery from the main platform and then walk through the museum-quality displays that detail the area's unique human and natural history. Finally, your motorcoach will depart down the scenic Klondike Highway. Take in spectacular views of waterfalls and alpine valleys as you make your way back to the ship.
highlights: Ascend into the mountains by train, following the same trail taken by miners more than a century ago.
Enjoy a short photo stop at the historic Yukon Territory sign.
Experience the exhilaration of crossing a 57-foot high suspension bridge.
Enjoy a hearty bowl of bison chili and homemade rolls.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Adventure Zipline Tour
commerceProducts.description: Soar over British Columbia's majestic west coast rainforest on an exhilarating zipline ride. Professionally trained guides will emphasize safety and comfort before you glide 37 miles per hour at 150 feet off the ground. The eight zip lines range from 150 feet to an unforgettable 1000 feet in length. During your descent through the forest and over the Sooke Hills, your guides will share interesting facts and stories about the local ecology and wildlife.
highlights: Soar up to 150 feet off the ground on eight ziplines ranging from 150 to1000 feet in length.
Professionally trained guides monitor your zipline experience, ensuring your safety, comfort, and enjoyment.
Reach the access point that descends across 100 acres of West Coast Rainforest via a short ATV ride.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Gold Creek Salmon Bake
commerceProducts.description: Treat yourself to a delicious taste of traditional Alaskan cuisine with an authentic salmon bake, as you soak in the natural beauty of Juneau, dine on freshly grilled fish and even try your hand at gold panning.
highlights: A short but scenic shuttle ride brings you to Gold Creek, one of Juneau’s beautiful waterways, where you settle beneath the natural rainforest canopy and savor the enticing aroma of wild salmon grilling over fragrant alder wood.

Groove to the tunes of live music, as you enjoy an Alaskan all-you-can-eat feast that includes all the regional fixings, from barbecue ribs, cornbread, au gratin potatoes, coleslaw and blueberry cake to the star of the show – wild caught and freshly grilled salmon.

After your delicious meal, venture down a nature trail to a nearby, picturesque waterfall, where you can admire the historical ruins of the Wagner Gold Mine. Round out your authentic Alaskan experience by sifting for some gold or roasting a marshmallow over an open camp fire, before returning to your awaiting ship.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Mendenhall Lake Kayak and Salmon Bake
commerceProducts.description: Experience the wonder and majesty of the Mendenhall Glacier on this once-in-a-lifetime kayak journey to Mendenhall Lake, followed by a delicious meal at the Gold Creek Salmon Bake. Enjoy a narrated coach ride to the breathtaking Mendenhall Lake. Here, you’ll meet your guide, receive all equipment and instruction, and begin your kayak adventure. Be one of the select few to paddle across this incredible lake, passing stunning icebergs and viewing Alaskan wildlife in its natural habitat. Venture within a safe distance of the massive, awe-inspiring face of the Mendenhall Glacier. See the breathtaking Nugget Falls. Then, after your thrilling journey, you’ll transfer to Gold Creek Salmon Bake for an all-you-can-eat feast set in the Alaskan rainforest. With a marshmallow roasting pit, majestic waterfall, and mining relics, the Gold Creek Salmon Bake has something for everyone.
highlights: Enjoy a salmon feast at the Gold Creek Salmon Bake.
Venture within a safe distance of the massive and awe-inspiring Mendenhall Glacier.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Dog Sled Summer Camp
commerceProducts.description: Ready for an only in Alaska experience? Take a scenic drive along Alaska’s beautiful Gastineau Channel into remote areas and be greeted by professional Mushers and a team of Alaskan Huskies. You will receive an educational tour of the camp, and visit a 19th century replica Iditarod Outpost. Finally, climb aboard a wheeled dogcart and experience the thrill of mushing through Sheep Creek Valley behind a team of highly trained Alaskan Huskies.
highlights: Meet a professional  Musher and a team of Alaskan huskies at the Dog Sled Summer Camp at AJ Mine.
Hold and pet the puppies, and take part in their initial social training, while learning about dog healthcare.
Mush approximately 1.5 miles through Sheep Creek Valley behind a team of highly trained Alaskan Huskies.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Alaska Sled Dogs and Musher's Camp
commerceProducts.description: Experience a true Alaskan adventure while partaking in the official state sport. Take a sled ride through the lush rainforest, play with husky puppies, and learn about this unique element of Alaska’s culture. You’ll join in the summer training of an expert team of sled dogs when you ride a custom-designed wheeled summertime sled. Then a dog handler will share knowledge about the equipment, dog care, training, and efforts required by this sport. You’ll also get a chance to enjoy the scenery that lured you to Alaska as you walk across a suspension bridge overlooking Fish Creek. Finally, cuddle up and help socialize the adorable little huskies.
highlights: Learn about dog sledding - Alaska's official state sport.
Play with adorable Alaskan pups following your mush ride.
Feel your heart race on this thrilling mush across the vast Alaskan wilderness.

commerceProducts.description: Get picture-perfect shots of Alaska's towering natural beauty on a photo safari with a  Knowledgeable Photographer, who takes you off the beaten path to the hidden gems of Ketchikan.
highlights: Join your guide, an Alaskan photographer, and visit multiple sites that are perfect for taking iconic photos of the Ketchikan area.
If you’re already an expert, you can take advantage of insider knowledge on the best locations, and if you’re a novice photographer, learn the best ways to capture incredible  photos.
Explore Ketchikan's most photogenic spots, like rushing water and flowing water sites, broad mountains, Cape Fox Totem, and Bar Harbor.
Relax and review your images over a piping hot cup of coffee and delicious cookies at Potlatch Totem Parks & Gifts.
Last, stop at the North Tongass Ocean for one last chance to shoot before returning to the port.

commerceProducts.title: Hop-On Hop-Off City Sightseeing
commerceProducts.description: Tap into your inner explorer with this British Columbia shore excursion, as you hop aboard a double-decker bus and explore Victoria's charming landmarks at your leisure.
highlights: Board your double-decker bus and begin your exploration of Victoria - with four stops to choose from and informative audio commentary, the magic of this capital city is your to discover.

Hop off at Cook Street Village, a charming shopping district where you can stroll along tree-lined streets, browse bustling boutiques and take in the relaxed atmosphere at one of the area's many cozy cafe's.

Feeling hungry? Stop at Victoria's Chinatown, the oldest in the counter, and wander the bustling neighborhood, as it comes alive with traditional Chinese architecture and the delicious aroma of fresh Asian cuisine.

Eager for a bit of culture? Pause at the iconic Empress Hotel, a luxurious four-star hotel with chateau-style architecture and a long history of distinguished guests. Just across the street, take in the beauty of the Inner Harbour, a scenic patch of waterfront.
Even more picturesque waterfront views await at the Fisherman's Wharf, which features lively restaurants, brightly-colored floating houses and a unique glimpse into Victoria's maritime heritage.

Once you've satisfied your curiosity, feel free to stay downtown and discover the capital city even further, or bring your Sightseeing City excursion to a close with a bus ride back to you awaiting ship.

activityLevel.description: Full participation may require leisurely walking over primarily even surfaces. There may be a limited number of steps, a minimal amount of cobblestones or uneven surfaces, and some standing for extended periods of time.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Whale Watching and Lighthouse Adventure
commerceProducts.description: Embark on a whale watching adventure to the historic Sentinel Island Lighthouse in a custom built luxury whale watching vessel. Relax in a heated cabin with outside viewing areas while spotting Humpback whales and other marine life. Upon arrival at the lighthouse, enjoy a short hike on a scenic boardwalk, explore the lighthouse that was built in 1902, and take in the views of the surrounding islands. Next, watch more marine wildlife while enroute to the harbor where your driver-guide will make one final stop at Brotherhood Bridge which offers a stunning view of the Mendenhall Glacier**, before you return to your ship.
highlights: Enjoy a narrated tour to the whale watching harbor.
Watch whales in a comfortable, luxury boats with heated cabins, snacks, and beverages.
Arrive and explore the lighthouse building, property, picturesque boardwalk and views of Lynn Canal and surrounding islands.
Return to the vessel and continue watching whales, sea lions, and bald eagles en-route to the harbor.
Meet up with your driver/guide for a narrated return tour to your ship with a stop at Brotherhood Bridge for a scenic view of the Mendenhall Glacier.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Alaskan Bear Encounter by Land and Sea
commerceProducts.description: Experience the massive splendor of Alaskan wilderness as you boat to Neets Bay Observatory to see wild bears in their natural habitat, feasting on salmon in a pristine, seldom-visited environment.
highlights: Enjoy Alaska's sublime natural beauty on a narrated drive to Clover Pass Resort to join an experienced captain and crew on a boat setting sail along the magnificent coast, passing Tongass National Forest on your way to Neets Bay Observatory, a pristine wilderness area that is limited to a small number of visitors each year, including you.

Enjoy the serenity of the verdant forest as you walk along a well-maintained forest trail to a viewing platform and listen as your guide discusses the resident bear population, the surrounding forest, and the importance of hatcheries to enhance salmon production in southeast Alaska.
Have your camera ready for the once-in-a-lifetime experience of co-existing with these incredible creatures as the bears teach their young to fish or relax after a big meal of fresh salmon.
After, board the boat for the return trip and another glimpse of Last Frontier scenery before returning to the port to meet your awaiting ship.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Rainforest Canopy and Zipline Adventure
commerceProducts.description: Glide through the top of a rainforest canopy along a series of cables suspended between tall hemlock, spruce, and cedars and gain a unique perspective on the rainforest surrounds. This adventure excursion combines a flight over 2400 feet  of high tension cable, guaranteed to thrill all.
highlights: Depart the pier for the brief transfer to Herring Cove, located within the borders of the magnificent Tongass National Forest. This major rainforest reserve embraces one of Alaska's richest salmon spawning streams and a pristine inter-tidal estuary. Abundant wildlife habitat here includes a high concentration of bald eagles, black bear, and a variety of other species.
At Herring Cove, an all-terrain custom 4x4 vehicle transports you up a steep rain forest hillside to the canopy chalet.

Professionally trained guides outfit you with state-of-the-art safety equipment and provide a detailed orientation before you mount the first platform. Your rainforest canopy challenge features a flight over nine ziplines, an aerial bridge suspended between tall spruce, hemlock and cedar trees, and ground-based nature trails.
Your guide will progressively ease you into the forest canopy along cables ranging from a short 100 feet up to 600 feet with platform as high as 100 feet above the ground.
Eagles and bears are often seen from the platforms.
Cross a hanging bridge that will lead you to our longest zip that breaks out into the canopy offering breathtaking views of the forest and salmon spawning creek below.
Throughout this adventure, you will be surrounded by the sights and sounds of the rain forest wildlife preserve.

The final zip-line emerges from the rain forest and transports you over lush grassy wetlands to a viewing tower overlooking the Whitman Lake Fish Hatchery, 55 feet above the ground where you will gently descend to the forest floor.

Time permitting, grab a freshly brewed coffee or a cold Alaskan beer and explore our Tsimshian totem park, see a master carver working, check out our historic sawmill and watch our local blacksmiths at work.
Stop by the General Store to see photos taken during your zip-line experience and to shop for mementos of your Alaska adventure.
Your excursion concludes with a scenic drive back to the ship.

activityLevel.description: This tour is not recommended for guests with pre-existing medical conditions such as cardiac, respiratory, or orthopedic conditions, including joint injuries or disease, or any other serious medical condition. Guests must consider their physical fitness level and medical history when determining whether this tour is appropriate. commerceProducts.title: The Local Table - Hands on Salmon Cooking Class
commerceProducts.description: Immerse yourself in the rich flavors and traditions of Alaska with a hands-on wild salmon cooking shore excursion along the picturesque Tongass Narrows.
highlights: Begin your culinary journey with a short walk to The Local Table – a cozy venue overlooking the serene waters of the Tongass Narrows.
Greeted by your instructor, a third-generation fisherman with deep ties to Alaska’s fishing heritage, feel right at home as the warm aromas of fresh seafood and spices fill the air.
Under the expert guidance of your instructor, dive into the preparation of three simple yet extraordinary salmon dishes: classic grilled salmon, smoked salmon dip, and salmon cakes with two sauces.
The class features hands-on cooking stations, where you chop, marinate, and assemble alongside fellow participants.
Once the cooking is complete, gather around the communal dining table to enjoy the fruits of your labor.

The Tongass Narrows provides a stunning natural backdrop as you indulge in your homemade meal. Watch as float planes glide over the water, fishing boats return to the harbor, and wildlife, like seals or eagles, make the occasional appearance.
After your meal, conclude your Hands on Salmon Cooking Class with a satisfied palate, a wealth of new culinary skills, and a recipe booklet to recreate the dishes at home.

commerceProducts.title: Explore Wild Adventure Yukon, Sled Dogs and Scenic Highway
commerceProducts.description: Embark on a thrilling adventure that combines history, stunning landscapes, and the spirit of the gold rush as you journey up the legendary White Pass Summit. Your experience begins with a comfortable, narrated motor coach ride through the charming streets of downtown Skagway, where you’ll pass historic wooden boardwalks and the preserved façades of this once-bustling frontier town. As you leave the town behind, you're driver guide will share stories of Skagway's colorful history while you retrace the steps of the brave prospectors who risked everything in search of fortune during the Klondike Gold Rush. As you climb the summit, 3,292 feet above sea level, you’ll be awe-struck by breathtaking views of the rugged landscape, sweeping vistas of snow-capped peaks and glacial-fed rivers.
highlights: Enjoy a guided drive through downtown Skagway and up the White Pass Summit, hearing stories of Skagway settlers during the Gold Rush.
Indulge in an all-you-can-eat Alaskan BBQ chicken buffet with all the fixings.
Enjoy a variety of activities fun for the whole family at Wild Adventure Yukon, including a sled dog experience.

activityLevel.description: Full participation may require leisurely walking over primarily even surfaces. There may be a limited number of steps, a minimal amount of cobblestones or uneven surfaces, and some standing for extended periods of time.
activityLevel.name: Mild

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Potlatch Park and Lumberjack Show
commerceProducts.description: Experience the rich native history of Ketchikan and enjoy a taste of the enduring spirit that pioneered this Alaska’s first wilderness city. Your fun-filled day begins in hardy fashion at the Great Alaskan Lumberjack Show. Enjoy a taste of pioneer days as you watch logging history come to life in the form of competitions, exhibitions and awesome displays of rugged skill. From here, you’ll head to the spectacular Potlach Totem Park, where Ketchikan’s native culture is celebrated in grand fashion. Enjoy a guided tour through this fascinating collection of totem poles and witness the tradition tools used to create these timeless reflections of Native Alaska. Afterwards, relax on a narrated drive that winds along the Ketchikan waterfront, and showcases the salmon canneries along the North Tongass Highway. Discover Ketchikan on a tour that reveals the heart and soul of Alaska’s first city. THIS TOUR IS OPERATED BY A TOUR OPERATOR THAT HAS BEEN THIRD-PARTY VERIFIED TO AN INTERNATIONALLY RECOGNIZED SUSTAINABILITY STANDARD.
highlights: Witness the Great Alaskan Lumberjack show for a glimpse of hard working pioneer days.
Enjoy a narrated drive along Ketchikan’s waterfront and through the former Red Light District.
Visit the Potlatch Totem Park and for a colorful look at Alaska’s artful native culture.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Hunting for Halibut
commerceProducts.description: No matter your skill level, you will fish like a local, working the sandy flats or sea floor pinnacles with your rod in hand, drifting with the current and jigging in waters as deep as 300 feet. Species availability changes throughout the season, and daily factors such as tides and wind affect your fishing opportunities. This multi-species Alaskan fishing expedition allows you to head out with the confidence that you will catch fish. Down there in the twilight of the deep, rockfish, cod, flounder, sharks, skates, and mighty halibut find their way to your bait. No matter what the conditions or the catch, this trip offers the best chance of exposing you to the strange and alien species found on the Southeast Alaskan deep-sea floor. Historically 50% of our guests catch Pacific Halibut. 2026 updated regulations: Daily bag limit of one halibut per guest. Max lower size limit of 34”. As well, there is an upper limit fixed at 80” or greater for all 2026 dates.
highlights: Deliver your halibut and a purchase of local Alaskan seafood shipment to your home for an additional charge.
Pursue the elusive halibut with an experienced guide while learning the secrets of working the tides.
Board a Raider Pro Fisherman and experience an anglers dream come true - fishing for Pacific halibut in Alaska.
